title: SUPPORT GROUPS - 211info
text: FIRST STEPS CENTER FOR AUTISM AND DISABILITIES SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups, social opportunities, events, workshops, and resources for individuals and families experiencing neurodivergence. Accepts volunteers. 228 S Main Avenue Warrenton, OR 97146 Eligibility: Teen Group: Neurodivergent teens, ages 13-19 Adult Group: Neurodivergent adults, age 18 and older Parents and Caregivers Group: Parents and caregivers of neurodivergent individuals Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Teen Group: Friday 7pm Adult Group: 3rd Thursday of the month 6pm Parents and Caregivers Group: 2nd Thursday of the month 6pm Email (503) 861-8104 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 861-8104 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website, or email for more information. Appointment only outside of normal business hours. Some groups require preregistration. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Clatsop County Services: Disability Related Parenting Programs * Autism Spectrum Disorder Health/Disability Related Social Clubs * Autism Spectrum Disorder Health/Disability Related Support Groups Health/Disability Related Support Groups * Autism Spectrum Disorder Parent Support Groups * Autism Spectrum Disorder Volunteer Opportunities * Autism Spectrum Disorder If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. FIRST STEPS CENTER FOR AUTISM AND DISABILITIES
